commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=147f6c4946a6afae1cabd1aaf3ecb1fc51dda740)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Codex bundled plugin to return portable MessagePresentation picker buttons for `/codex` and related menu commands, adds plugin enable/disable target pickers, and extends Codex and Telegram callback-limit tests.

PR surface: Source +271, Tests +104. Total +375 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection and PR-body terminal output show the new command paths, while live Telegram proof remains missing for the visible transport path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is the right next step because the remaining blockers are current-head Telegram proof, docs alignment, and maintainer acceptance of the `/codex` default behavior change rather than a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(codex): add portable Codex command pickers
target.body:
## Summary

- Codex now owns the native slash-command picker tree for `/codex`, `/codex plugins menu`, `/codex fast menu`, `/codex permissions menu`, and `/codex computer-use menu`.
- Picker replies use portable `MessagePresentation` buttons whose values are real slash commands. Channel plugins render those buttons through their existing presentation support when possible.
- Telegram-specific Codex callback state was removed. Telegram now uses the generic presentation button renderer and existing synthetic command callback path, including the existing callback length filter.
- `/codex plugins enable` and `/codex plugins disable` without a target now return Codex-owned target pickers. Leaf mutations still go through the existing owner/operator.admin auth gate.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Integrations
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #82218
- Related #82219, #83293

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/codex` and related Codex menu commands expose portable native slash-command pickers from the Codex command owner, while Telegram maps those presentations through the generic channel renderer instead of Codex-specific Telegram callbacks.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, built OpenClaw CLI/runtime reporting `OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (147f6c4)`, branch head `147f6c4946a6afae1cabd1aaf3ecb1fc51dda740`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the built `dist/extensions/codex/index.js` plugin entry through its registered command handler with a live runtime config shape containing one enabled and one disabled Codex sub-plugin, then ran focused regression checks.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the built OpenClaw Codex plugin command surface:

```text
$ /codex
Codex commands. Pick a category or type:

  1. /codex plugins menu
  2. /codex permissions menu
  3. /codex fast menu
buttons=["/codex plugins menu","/codex permissions menu","/codex fast menu","/codex computer-use menu","/codex account","/codex help"]
$ /codex plugins menu
Codex sub-plugins. Pick a sub-action or type:

  1. /codex plugins list
  2. /codex plugins enable
  3. /codex plugins disable
buttons=["/codex plugins list","/codex plugins enable","/codex plugins disable","/codex plugins help","/codex"]
$ /codex plugins enable
Codex sub-plugins to enable. Pick one or type:

  1. /codex plugins enable calendar

Type '/codex plugins menu' to go back to the plugins menu.
buttons=["/codex plugins enable calendar","/codex plugins menu"]
$ /codex plugins disable
Codex sub-plugins to disable. Pick one or type:

  1. /codex plugins disable notion

Type '/codex plugins menu' to go back to the plugins menu.
buttons=["/codex plugins disable notion","/codex plugins menu"]
```

Supplemental checks: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/button-types.test.ts` passed 138 tests; `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` reported no whitespace errors; autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
Observed result after fix: `/codex` returns a portable presentation with real command values for plugins, permissions, fast, computer-use, account, and help; `/codex plugins menu` routes through Codex-owned plugin picker logic; `/codex plugins enable` and `disable` without a target render effective-state target pickers; Telegram presentation buttons drop overlong callback values instead of emitting invalid Telegram callback data.
What was not tested: live Telegram interaction after this refactor. The earlier Telegram screenshots in this PR body described the removed in-place `cdx_*` callback design and are no longer the implementation contract.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
- Target tests:
  - `extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/button-types.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Codex owns menu routing and picker command values; plugin enable/disable pickers filter by effective state; Telegram generic presentation rendering keeps valid command callbacks and drops overlong values.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Typing `/codex` with no args returns a picker instead of status text. Status remains available through `/codex status`.
- New typed menu entry points: `/codex plugins menu`, `/codex fast menu`, `/codex permissions menu`, and `/codex computer-use menu`.
- Typing `/codex plugins enable` or `/codex plugins disable` without a plugin name now shows a picker instead of usage text.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Picker buttons carry the same slash commands a user could type manually. Mutating plugin commands still execute through `handleCodexPluginsSubcommand` and the existing owner/operator.admin check.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Mostly`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

The only intentional behavior shift is `/codex` no-arg returning the menu picker. Existing explicit commands, including `/codex status`, remain available.
